# Git Commit Skill Overview

## Purpose
This skill handles the staging and committing of changes in a Git repository. It ensures meaningful commits with appropriate messages and proper staging of files.

## Key Features
- **Smart Staging**: Stage all changes or specific files based on user intent
- **Status Checking**: Always verify what's being committed before finalizing
- **Message Crafting**: Help create meaningful commit messages following best practices
- **Commit Verification**: Confirm successful commits and show commit details
- **Partial Commits**: Support for committing only specific files or changes

## When to Use
- Saving work progress with version control
- Creating checkpoints in development
- Preparing changes for pushing to remote repositories
- Organizing changes into logical commits

## Best Practices
- Write clear, descriptive commit messages
- Use imperative mood ("Add feature" not "Added feature")
- Keep commits focused on single logical changes
- Review staged changes before committing
- Include relevant context in commit messages

## Prerequisites
- Git repository must be initialized
- User must have changes to commit
- Git user.name and user.email should be configured
